articles.description: Israel launched a daylight attack Saturday on Iran's capital, with a cloud of smoke rising from the city's downtown near the offices of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
articles.title: Live updates: Israel confirms attack on Iran as explosions are heard in Tehran

articles.author: The Associated Press
articles.description: The U.S. and Israel struck Iran on Saturday. President Donald Trump said the Iranian public should “take over" their government. The first strikes appeared to target Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
articles.title: Live updates: Iran’s foreign minister says supreme leader and president are alive, 'as far as I know'

articles.description: Oil traders brace for big moves next week as the fallout from U.S. and Israeli strikes on Middle East oil supply stays unclear. On Friday, Brent crude closes at a seven-month high of $72.87 on war fears. Analysts say prices will calm down if shipping and key sites stay safe. Disruption of Iranian exports could tighten global supply. Iran ships about 1.6 million barrels a day, mostly to China. Another risk is the Strait of Hormuz, which handles about 20% of global oil. Experts say Iran has little reason to close it.
articles.title: Oil prices set for swings as US and Israeli strikes impact Middle East

articles.description: The U.S. and Israel’s attack on Iran has disrupted air travel across the Middle East and beyond. The United Arab Emirates and Israel have closed their airspace. Qatar Airways says it has canceled flights to and from Doha because of Qatari airspace closure. Airlines are rerouting planes that were already heading to Israel. KLM said earlier in the week that it was suspending flights to and from Tel Aviv starting Sunday. Turkish Airlines has suspended many regional routes through Monday.
articles.title: Attack on Iran disrupts Middle East air travel

articles.author: Brian Melley
articles.description: The U.S. and Israel have launched major attacks on Iran that President Donald Trump says aim to crush Iran’s military and stop production of a nuclear weapon. Iran’s Revolutionary Guard said it fired drones and missiles at Israel and strikes aimed at U.S. military installations in Bahrain, Kuwait and Qatar. Several countries closed their airspace as explosions were heard across the region. Smoke was seen rising in Tehran near offices tied to Iran’s supreme leader. Officials report little damage in Israel and no known injuries. Trump posted a video urging Iranians to take cover and later rise up and take control of their government.
articles.title: US and Israel launch major attack on Iran amid nuclear tensions

articles.description: Israel launched an attack on Iran on Saturday, with the country’s defense minister declaring a state of emergency across the country. Israel Katz made the announcement as thick smoke rose from an explosion in downtown Tehran, Iran’s capital. Iranian state television acknowledged the blast, without providing more details. Sirens sounded across Israel at the same time. The Israeli military said that it had issued a “proactive alert to prepare the public for the possibility of missiles being launched toward the state of Israel.”
articles.title: Israel's defense minister says his country has attacked Iran and declares a state of emergency

articles.author: The Associated Press
articles.description: U.S. President Donald Trump said in an 8-minute video posted on his Truth Social that the U.S. has begun “major combat operations in Iran.”
articles.title: Read President Trump’s statement on Iran in full

articles.description: The Revolutionary Guard said in a statement that in the first phase of retaliation, named “Truthful Promise 4,” Iran’s military struck the command of the U.S. 5th Fleet in Bahrain as well as U.S. bases in Qatar and the United Arab Emirates, and military targets in Israel.
articles.title: Smoke seen in Bahrain, Qatar and Kuwait as Iran retaliates against strikes

articles.description: U.S. President Donald Trump urged the Iranian people to "take over your government" in a video address posted Saturday.
articles.title: Trump urges Iranians to 'take over your government' as US and Israel launch attack on Iran

articles.description: Scenes from Tel Aviv as strikes are intercepted following the US-Israel attack on Iran.
articles.title: WATCH: Interceptions seen from Tel Aviv after US-Israel attack on Iran
